div1.entryFree: Bookish, given to books, more acquainted with books than with men and things: whose b. rule hath pulled fair England down, H6B I, 1, 259. the b. theoric, Oth. I, 1, 24. == lettered: though I am not b., yet I can read waiting-gentlewoman in the scape, Wint. III, 3, 73 (the shepherd's speech).
